jokes.text: What are you touching? No reason to touch.
jokes.setup: Casual conversation about restaurant investment
jokes.punchline: Larry's abrupt, socially inappropriate objection to casual touching
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry social awkwardness - perfectly in character and economical

jokes.text: Larry, you are so rude.
jokes.setup: Larry's behavior during dinner with friends
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's blunt assessment after they leave
jokes.explanation: Sets up the premise but not particularly funny on its own

jokes.text: Well, I smelled her perfume. It's hard not to notice.
jokes.setup: Cheryl asking if Larry even noticed Mindy
jokes.punchline: Larry's defensive response that he noticed her perfume
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's obliviousness while trying to seem attentive

jokes.text: He runs to the bathroom every time the check comes.
jokes.setup: Discussion about not picking up checks
jokes.punchline: Larry exposing Stu's bathroom trick
jokes.explanation: Relatable social observation that sets up later payoff

jokes.text: Yeah, it's our third child.
jokes.setup: Larry's precious attitude toward his car
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's sarcastic exaggeration of Larry's attachment
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - one line captures Larry's absurd priorities

jokes.text: When you said 'Mr. Duplicity,' I thought you were referring to me...
jokes.setup: Discussion about Alanis Morissette's mysterious ex
jokes.punchline: Larry's paranoid assumption it's about him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry narcissism and paranoia in one line

jokes.text: She'll tell me. People confide in me. Everybody opens up to me.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Alanis keeping her song subject secret
jokes.punchline: Larry's delusional self-assessment given his social incompetence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dramatic irony - audience knows Larry is terrible with people

jokes.text: I'll go to the little fat boy's room.
jokes.setup: Stu finishing the 'Fat Boy's Special' meal
jokes.punchline: Playing on 'little boy's room' with the meal name
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay but nothing special

jokes.text: I said don't pick up the check!
jokes.setup: Earlier discussion about Stu's bathroom trick
jokes.punchline: Larry's frustration at being stuck with the bill again
jokes.explanation: Good callback that pays off the setup

jokes.text: There is going to be a terrorist attack this weekend in LA.
jokes.setup: Wanda's serious buildup about CIA information
jokes.punchline: The absurd casual delivery of apocalyptic news
jokes.explanation: Brilliant premise setup - the casual delivery of outrageous information drives the entire episode

jokes.text: Why don't we call the terrorists and ask them could they pick another weekend more suited for you?
jokes.setup: Cheryl's concern about the NRDC benefit timing
jokes.punchline: Larry's sarcastic suggestion to negotiate with terrorists
jokes.explanation: Excellent Larry logic - treating terrorists like inconvenient party planners. Great structure and quotability.

jokes.text: Why don't we call the terrorists and ask them could they pick another weekend more suited for you?
jokes.setup: Cheryl worrying about the NRDC benefit timing
jokes.punchline: Larry's sarcastic suggestion to negotiate with terrorists
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David logic - treating terrorism like a scheduling conflict

jokes.text: Then at least, you know, one of us would... survive.
jokes.setup: Discussion about staying together during potential attack
jokes.punchline: Larry's pragmatic approach to their potential deaths
jokes.explanation: Dark but perfectly in character - Larry's twisted practical logic about survival

jokes.text: Almost seems a little... selfish that you would want both of us to... perish.
jokes.setup: Cheryl suggesting they should go together
jokes.punchline: Larry turning mutual death into an act of selfishness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - Larry making Cheryl the villain for wanting to die together. Perfect twisted Larry logic.

jokes.text: But hopefully I could at some point get back some semblance of a life.
jokes.setup: Larry admitting it would be difficult without Cheryl
jokes.punchline: His casual optimism about moving on from her death
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry selfishness delivered with deadpan understatement

jokes.text: But hopefully I could at some point get back some semblance of a life.
jokes.setup: Larry discussing surviving without Cheryl
jokes.punchline: His casual optimism about moving on from her death
jokes.explanation: Larry's matter-of-fact approach to grief is hilariously tone-deaf

jokes.text: They're not going to Orange County, the terrorists. They have nothing in Orange County.
jokes.setup: Mindy worried about her mother in Orange County
jokes.punchline: Larry's dismissive assessment of Orange County's strategic value
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observational comedy about Orange County's perceived irrelevance

jokes.text: They're not going to Orange County, the terrorists. They have nothing in Orange County.
jokes.setup: Mindy worrying about her 80-year-old mother
jokes.punchline: Larry's dismissive assessment of Orange County's terror appeal
jokes.explanation: Good geographical humor with Larry's casual dismissal of Orange County

jokes.text: I was driving around, I saw your shop, I thought, 'I've got to tell Mindy.'
jokes.setup: Mindy asking why Larry told her about the attack
jokes.punchline: Larry's transparent lie about his motivations
jokes.explanation: Larry's bad lying reveals his true selfish motives

jokes.text: I have really misjudged you. I can't thank you enough. You are an amazing person.
jokes.setup: Larry's selfish motivation for warning Mindy
jokes.punchline: Mindy's effusive gratitude for Larry's 'heroism'
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment as Larry gets credit for noble motives when his were selfish

jokes.text: This is before-shave.
jokes.setup: Mindy giving Larry aftershave samples
jokes.punchline: The nonsensical concept of 'before-shave'
jokes.explanation: Mildly absurd product concept that works as a throwaway gag

jokes.text: Larry David... apologize? That's very... unusual, yes, highly unusual, yes.
jokes.setup: Larry claiming he went to apologize to Mindy
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's shock at Larry apologizing
jokes.explanation: Good character beat establishing Larry's reputation

jokes.text: I feel like I apologize to somebody every day. Who says 'I'm sorry' more than me?
jokes.setup: Cheryl's amazement at Larry apologizing
jokes.punchline: Larry's delusional self-assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry delusion - audience knows he's the least apologetic person

jokes.text: Some time this weekend. You'll notice Alanis Morissette is not here.
jokes.setup: Larry and Cheryl arriving at the empty benefit
jokes.punchline: Stu's dry observation about the missing star performer
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery of the obvious consequences

jokes.text: It sounds like it might've started with Mindy Reiser. And guess who told Mindy?
jokes.setup: The revelation that word spread about the terrorist attack
jokes.punchline: The dramatic reveal tracing it back to Larry
jokes.explanation: Well-structured reveal that pays off the episode's central premise

jokes.text: I felt bad because I snubbed Mindy at dinner the other night, I didn't talk to her and l... Told her there'd be a terrorist attack in Los Angeles this weekend?
jokes.setup: Larry explaining his reasoning
jokes.punchline: His own realization of how insane his solution was
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to opening social awkwardness with escalated consequences

jokes.text: Why do you think we don't talk to her? She has a big mouth!
jokes.setup: Larry's defense of telling Mindy
jokes.punchline: Jeff revealing they avoid Mindy precisely because she can't keep secrets
jokes.explanation: Good escalation revealing Larry's complete misunderstanding of the social dynamics

jokes.text: I know what you do every time the check comes... you run to the bathroom.
jokes.setup: Earlier observation about Stu's bathroom trick
jokes.punchline: Larry using it as ammunition in their fight
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that escalates the confrontation

jokes.text: I invented that trick.
jokes.setup: Larry accusing Stu of the bathroom trick
jokes.punchline: Stu's proud claim of inventing the check-dodging technique
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - Stu's pride in his deceptive innovation

jokes.text: Everything's 'freaking' with this guy. He can't say the word fuck?
jokes.setup: Stu's use of 'freaking' as a euphemism
jokes.punchline: Larry's annoyance at the sanitized swearing
jokes.explanation: Good character observation about linguistic pet peeves

jokes.text: You should think about how much money you just lost for the NRDC.
jokes.setup: Larry making light comments while gargling
jokes.punchline: Cheryl pointing out the real consequences
jokes.explanation: Brings focus back to actual consequences of Larry's actions

jokes.text: N-E-S-T-L-E-S Nestle's makes the very best... Sorr-ry.
jokes.setup: Larry gargling in the bathroom
jokes.punchline: Interrupting the jingle with an apology through the gargle
jokes.explanation: Mild physical comedy with advertising jingle absurdity

jokes.text: My kids could've been wiped out. They go to school.
jokes.setup: Person complaining Larry didn't warn them about attack
jokes.punchline: The dramatic language about school attendance
jokes.explanation: Shows escalating consequences of Larry's secret-telling

jokes.text: Hey, big mouth!
jokes.setup: Wanda arriving to confront Larry
jokes.punchline: Direct insult about Larry's inability to keep secrets
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - two words capture Larry's fundamental flaw

jokes.text: It would've been nice if there was a small explosion, where nobody got hurt. A little firecracker or something.
jokes.setup: Mindy complaining about their ruined weekend
jokes.punchline: Larry wishing for a minor terrorist attack to vindicate him
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - Larry wanting just enough terrorism to prove he was right. Perfect character logic.

jokes.text: Larry! Oh my God, Larry, how are you?
jokes.setup: Casual encounter with celebrity
jokes.punchline: Martin Short's exaggerated enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Standard celebrity cameo setup

jokes.text: No, that sounds like Miss Piggy.
jokes.setup: Larry attempting Martin's character voice
jokes.punchline: Martin's critique of Larry's impression
jokes.explanation: Standard celebrity cameo humor

jokes.text: No, now you've had a stroke.
jokes.setup: Larry's continued bad impression attempts
jokes.punchline: Martin's harsh assessment escalating to medical condition
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the impression critique

jokes.text: Me too, I've been urinating all day.
jokes.setup: Larry expressing excitement about meeting Alanis
jokes.punchline: Alanis's bizarre overshare about her nervous response
jokes.explanation: Unexpected and uncomfortable overshare that fits the show's cringe sensibility

jokes.text: You've got to be kidding me?! They invited the Reisers and not us?
jokes.setup: Stu seeing Larry and Cheryl at the rescheduled benefit with the Reisers
jokes.punchline: Stu's outrage at being excluded after the earlier conflict
jokes.explanation: Good payoff showing the social consequences of the earlier fight

jokes.text: So that's who it is. I'll see you later.
jokes.setup: Larry promising to keep Alanis's secret about 'You Oughta Know'
jokes.punchline: His immediate casual revelation that he now knows
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry move - immediately betraying confidence while acting casual about it

jokes.text: Freak you! Freak you!
jokes.setup: Earlier mockery of Stu saying 'freaking'
jokes.punchline: Stu's continued use of sanitized profanity in anger
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Stu's consistent character trait

jokes.text: I know who this song is about.
jokes.setup: Alanis performing 'You Oughta Know' after confiding in Larry
jokes.punchline: Larry's loud announcement to the crowd during the performance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry moment - immediately and publicly betraying a confidence at the worst possible time. Great episode climax.
